title: Cache Performance, Scope, Integrity, and Poisoning Defense · Release Evidence Brief: Ci Setup Node Package Cache
summary: A privileged release restores an executable cache written from a fork, accepts broad restore keys, hides cold-install failures, and attributes network time to compilation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci setup node package cache increment in cache threat and measurement policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use setup-node dependency caching without caching node_modules and disable automatic caching in privileged workflows when risk outweighs speed.
Restrict who can save or restore executable caches across fork, branch, event, workflow, and privilege boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example privileged release restores executable cache content written by a fork through a broad fallback key. Compare cold and warm time, key scope, save authority, poisoning risk, and immutable artifact separation. Competency probe: Use setup-node dependency caching without caching node_modules and disable automatic caching in privileged workflows when risk outweighs speed. A defensible solution applies Ci Setup Node Package Cache, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to cache threat and measurement policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-worked-gha-cache-performance-integrity-4
  - competency: ci-worked-gha-cache-performance-integrity-4
    artifact: cache-policy.yaml
    decision: worked gha cache performance integrity 4
    environment: GitHub-hosted ubuntu-24.04 runner on a cold cache
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      cache-content: npm-downloads-only
      key: os-arch-node24-lockhash-config
      restore-prefix: exact-trusted-branch
      writer: trusted-push-only
      untrusted-reader: bounded-read
      node-modules: excluded
      privileged-release-cache: disabled
      cold-run: scheduled
      measurement: queue-install-build-test-upload
    verify: compare cold warm and poisoned-key fixtures while checking writer event scope restored key and critical path
    failure: Reject cache as provenance, executable fork-writable caches, broad restore keys, node_modules reuse, or warm-only evidence.
    repair: Cache disposable inputs, bind keys and writers, isolate trust domains, retain cold runs, and measure the actual critical path.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
